source: Remediation sequence C1-C6 (fresh-eyes audit 2026-06-06); filed for later manual implementation. See docs/PROJECT_MAP.md and the approved plan.
title: Complete tier round-trip coverage: L2 contract (C2/debugging)
what_failed: C1 Phase A added L1/L3/in-memory round-trip contracts but L2 (organized_memory) write->read is untested - the one tier with no round-trip guard.
where_failed: testing/ - no L2 write->read contract; tools_memory.py memory_write/read(tier='l2').
why_likely: L2 needs Supabase, so it was skipped when the free offline contracts were written.
migration_plan: 1. Offline (free): assert memory_write(tier='l2') builds a row whose text is recoverable by the L2 read path, via a recording-Supabase double (like the L1 test).
2. Optional costly: a real Supabase L2 write->read marked @pytest.mark.costly for manual runs.
3. Add the offline part to the suite (consider the gate).
implementation_notes: Smart and simple: reuse the _RecordingSupabase pattern from test_memory_roundtrip_contract.py. No network in the default suite.
analysis: Completes tier coverage so the round-trip contract is honestly 'all tiers', not 'the offline ones'.
